nameMeanings.name: Boye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Olivier.  Scandinavian Erik.

North German and Danish: from an ancient Germanic personal name, Boio, possibly meaning ‘boy’ (see Boy 1) or, alternatively, representing a variant of Bothe , with the regular Low German loss of the dental between vowels. This surname is also found in Norway and Sweden. Compare Boie .
English: variant of   Boy  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Boykin
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name   Boye  .
History: Jarvis Boykin was one of the free planters who assented to the‘Fundamental Agreement’ of the New Haven Colony on June 4, 1639.

variants.name: Beu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang.
North German:
variant of   Boye  .

variants.name: Boysen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerd, Hans, Erwin, Hermann, Juergen, Lorenz, Siegfried.
North German, Danish, and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Boye  .

variants.name: Boie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Emile, Fernand.
North German and Danish: variant of   Boye  .
Americanized form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Bey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammed, Abdullah, Dawoud, Fatima, Jamaal, Rasheedah, Shakoor, Zaib, Aisha, Akil, Ameen, Aminah.
North German and Dutch: from the personal name Beye or Baye, vowel variants of Boye .
Germanized form of Sorbian Baj: nickname or occupational name from Upper Sorbian baj ‘storyteller’. Compare Bay .

variants.name: Boje
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Goetz, Johannes.
German: variant of   Boye  .

variants.name: Boyens
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Georg.
North German and North Frisian: patronymic from the personal name   Boye  .

variants.name: Boy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans Peter, Heinz, Horst.

English: from the Middle English personal name Boie, which may be from ancient Germanic Boio (see Boye 1 and 2) or an Old English Boia. The latter is common as a first element in English placenames, and is difficult to tell apart from the ordinary word boy, to which it may be related.
English: from Middle English boy ‘young man, servant’, also ‘knave’ or ‘rogue’. Compare Ladd .

variants.name: Boykins
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Boeijkens: patronymic from the personal name Boye with the diminutive element -ken and genitive -s. Compare the English cognate Boykin and North German Boyken .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: John Boye had at least 1 son with Agnes.

fr.lifeSummary: John Boye a eu au moins 1 fils avec Agnes.

pt.lifeSummary: John Boye teve pelo menos 1 filho com Agnes.

ru.lifeSummary: У John Boye был по крайней мере 1 сын от Agnes.

es.lifeSummary: John Boye tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Agnes.
